Regulatory Mar 19, 2026

DPE Class Action Targets FAA Termination Policies

Lawsuit alleges loss of appeal rights and procedural protections.

The FAA faces legal scrutiny over Designated Pilot Examiner termination procedures, potentially reshaping how the agency enforces regulatory compliance and examiner accountability across the certification system.

United Airlines To Debut 1st Premium-Heavy Boeing 787-9 From San Francisco Next Month

United Airlines unveils its most premium international aircraft, featuring a nose-to-tail layout of 99 premium seats.

United's 787-9 deployment with 99 premium seats from San Francisco signals a strategic shift toward premium-heavy international route profitability and market positioning against competitors on long-haul routes.

The USAF's planned May solicitation for KC-135 center console modernization represents a significant opportunity for avionics and cockpit systems suppliers to compete for upgrades on one of the military's most critical aging tanker platforms.

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Regulatory Mar 19, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

The FAA's 2024 SMS rule represents the aviation industry's fundamental shift from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating risks before they materialize.

The Striking Differences Passengers Notice Between The Airbus A380 & Boeing 747

Find out the key differences between the passenger experience on the Boeing 747 and A380.

While both aircraft dominate long-haul markets, the A380's double-deck configuration and larger cabin cross-section fundamentally reshape passenger comfort and operational economics compared to the 747's single-deck design.

Commercial orbital debris removal services represent an emerging aerospace market opportunity that addresses critical space sustainability challenges while creating new revenue streams for spacecraft technology providers.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

Press Release: British Airways’ first Starlink flight, a fitted 787, takes off

British Airways’ first aircraft equipped with Starlink Wi-Fi took to the skies today, the first flight operated by a UK airline featuring the innovative technology. The 787-8 aircraft bound for Houston is the first in the airline’s fleet to be connected, giving customers free access to fast, reli...

British Airways' deployment of Starlink-equipped aircraft represents a competitive differentiator in transatlantic service, with free high-speed connectivity becoming an operational and revenue-protection asset for premium carriers.

Military Mar 19, 2026

U.S. F-35 Makes Emergency Landing After Allegedly Being Hit by Iranian Fire

CENTCOM says a U.S. F-35 made an emergency landing at a U.S. air base in the Middle East after it “was struck by what is believed to be Iranian fire,” according to sources quoted by CNN. A U.S. F-35 Lightning II fighter jet was forced to make an emergency landing at a U.S. air base […]

Combat aircraft vulnerability to hostile fire necessitates enhanced defensive systems and operational protocols for military aviation assets deployed in contested airspace.

See The Aviationist →

Industry Mar 19, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through advanced technologies offers airlines and airports a pragmatic pathway to accommodate growing demand while simultaneously reducing environmental impact and congestion.

See MDPI →

Press Release: Egyptair’s new A350-900 to feature Panasonic IFE

EgyptAir's deployment of Panasonic's advanced IFEC systems on its new A350-900 strengthens the carrier's competitive positioning in long-haul markets while validating Panasonic's continued dominance in premium in-flight entertainment solutions.

United's enhanced 787-9 Dreamliner with elevated premium seating configuration represents a competitive differentiation strategy in long-haul international markets, targeting high-yield passengers with superior cabin experience.

American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center

An American bald eagle flies away from its nest and tree at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida on Friday, March 13, 2026. Bald eagle nesting surveys across NASA Kennedy, Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge, and Canaveral National Seashore are conducted annually to document the number of b...

NASA's Kennedy Space Center serves as critical habitat for protected species like bald eagles, demonstrating how aerospace facilities must balance launch operations with environmental stewardship and wildlife conservation.

As systems grow in scale, carrying millions of pounds of propellant, so too does the responsibility to fully understand the safety profile. Eng...

NASA's systematic collection of explosion data for advanced propulsion systems is critical for establishing safety protocols as commercial launch providers scale up liquid oxygen and methane rocket technologies.

Etihad Airways Announces Biggest US Expansion In History: 42% Flight Surge

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA Laser Reflecting Instrument Makes GPS Satellite More Accurate

A NASA laser reflecting technology that will aid Global Positioning System (GPS) accuracy is now operational as of March 9. The instrument, known as a laser retroreflector array, or LRA, launched aboard GPS III SV-09, the ninth of U.S. Space Force’s Block III Global Positioning System satellites,...

NASA's laser retroreflector array on GPS III SV-09 enhances satellite positioning accuracy, which directly improves navigation precision for all aviation operations relying on GPS-based systems.

See NASA →

Airlines demand EU leaders set 21-day notice for air traffic control strikes

An airline body representing some of Europe’s largest and most influential carriers is demanding that EU leaders set… The post Airlines demand EU leaders set 21-day notice for air traffic control strikes appeared first on AeroTime.

European airlines are seeking regulatory intervention to require advance strike notice from air traffic controllers, highlighting the operational vulnerability of the continent's carriers to labor disruptions in critical ground infrastructure.

Regulatory Mar 19, 2026

FCC Exempts Four Drones From Import Ban

March action follows January temporary exemptions tied to broader foreign UAS restrictions.

The FCC's selective drone import exemptions signal that regulatory bodies are creating case-by-case pathways for critical unmanned systems while maintaining broader foreign UAS restrictions, requiring operators to navigate evolving compliance frameworks.

Military Mar 19, 2026

A-10s Now Hunting Iranian Fast Attack Craft in the Strait of Hormuz

U.S. Air Force A-10s in maritime attack role are hunting small Iranian fast attack boats that could threaten shipping or naval forces in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Air Force A-10 Thundebolt II jets supporting operation Epic Fury have started flying a different type of mission. As explained by...

The U.S. Air Force is operationally adapting legacy A-10 Warthogs for maritime interdiction missions against asymmetric threats in strategically critical waterways, demonstrating the platform's continued relevance beyond traditional close air support roles.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking

NASA will provide live coverage of the launch and docking of a Roscosmos cargo spacecraft carrying about three tons of food, fuel, and supplies for the crew aboard the International Space Station. The unpiloted Roscosmos Progress 94 resupply spacecraft is scheduled to launch at 7:59 a.m. EDT (4:5...

NASA's continued reliance on Roscosmos cargo missions underscores the strategic importance of international space partnerships for maintaining ISS operations and highlights supply chain vulnerabilities that aviation and aerospace logistics must address.

A-10s Now Hunting Iranian Fast Attack Craft in the Strait of Hormuz

U.S. Air Force A-10s in maritime attack role are hunting small Iranian fast attack boats that could threaten shipping or naval forces in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Air Force A-10 Thundebolt II jets supporting operation Epic Fury have started flying a different type of mission. As explained by...

The U.S. Air Force is operationally adapting legacy A-10 Warthogs for maritime interdiction missions against asymmetric threats in strategically critical waterways, demonstrating the platform's continued relevance beyond traditional close air support roles.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking

NASA will provide live coverage of the launch and docking of a Roscosmos cargo spacecraft carrying about three tons of food, fuel, and supplies for the crew aboard the International Space Station. The unpiloted Roscosmos Progress 94 resupply spacecraft is scheduled to launch at 7:59 a.m. EDT (4:5...

NASA's continued reliance on Roscosmos cargo missions underscores the strategic importance of international space partnerships for maintaining ISS operations and highlights supply chain vulnerabilities that aviation and aerospace logistics must address.
